text.title: SEO for SPAs Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the SEO for SPAs methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Each route MUST declare rendering mode (SSG / SSR / ISR / CSR) per route map; CSR-only on marketing pages is forbidden.
rule.rationale: CSR pages are empty for crawlers on first byte; SSG/SSR/ISR ship content in initial HTML.

rule.statement: Every route MUST emit unique `<title>`, `<meta name='description'>`, `<link rel='canonical'>`, and OG tags; global-only metadata is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Duplicate titles fragment ranking signals; canonical resolves duplicate URL paths.

rule.statement: Each content template (article, product, recipe) MUST emit JSON-LD structured data matching schema.org type; absent or invalid structured data is rejected.
rule.rationale: Structured data unlocks rich snippets and clarifies content type to crawlers.

rule.statement: Multi-language sites MUST emit `<link rel='alternate' hreflang>` per locale including `x-default`; missing hreflang is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without hreflang, Google serves wrong locale to user, hurting CTR and confusing duplicate detection.

rule.statement: Site MUST ship `sitemap.xml` listing canonical URLs + `robots.txt`; auto-generation is preferred but the spec must enumerate paths included.
rule.rationale: Sitemap accelerates discovery and surfaces crawl errors; robots.txt prevents waste on auth pages.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare per-template Core Web Vitals targets (LCP<=2.5s, INP<=200ms, CLS<=0.1 at p75 mobile); CWV not declared is forbidden.
rule.rationale: CWV is a ranking factor; explicit targets focus optimisation work on real metrics.

rule.statement: Above-the-fold content MUST be present in initial HTML; pure client-only content above the fold is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Above-the-fold content that hydrates blocks LCP and crawlers; SSR/SSG keeps it visible immediately.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
